test.details: 581 passed; 0 failed; 3 ignored; 0 measured; finished in 0.16s

nonBlockingIssues.description: The 'cancelled' agent notification handler returns None (silently dropped). The framing layer correctly skips and logs a warning, but downstream features may need explicit handling.
try_decode_agent_response returns None for JSON-RPC error responses. Error responses are detected but not decoded as typed AgentResponse. The client lifecycle feature handles this at a higher layer.
MockTransport AsyncWrite::poll_write returns Ok(0) on lock contention, which appears as a successful zero-byte write. BufWriter above handles retries, so not a practical issue.
initialize() returns NotInitialized when state != Uninitialized (confusing error message when already initialized). Should return AlreadyInitialized or InvalidState.
session_list() does not enforce Authenticated state — a client in Initialized (pre-auth) state could call it. The agent would likely reject it but the client should enforce the auth requirement.
authenticate() does not enforce state == Initialized ordering. If called in Authenticated or Disconnected state, it proceeds with the request rather than rejecting.
subscribe() uses blocking_lock() as fallback, which can deadlock if called from within a tokio task that holds the mutex. Low risk since subscribe() is called from the main API surface.

AgentRequest handling in process_raw_line is not yet wired to the handlers module — agent requests are logged but not dispatched. Handlers are implemented and tested in isolation but runtime integration needs a follow-up step.

AutoApproveAll fallback when no 'allow' option exists sends a synthesized optionId='auto_reject' not from the request's options list. Could cause protocol error if agent strictly validates option IDs.

appliedUpdates.description: Added ACP client transport documentation: NDJSON framing, mock transport, client lifecycle, streaming event mapping, client-side handlers, and AgentPermissionPolicy details

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add note: The agent-client-protocol-schema crate uses #[serde(untagged)] on AgentResponse, causing deserialization ambiguity (AuthenticateResponse matches any JSON object). Workers implementing ACP request/response handling must use method-aware deserialization (track pending method names and deserialize accordingly) rather than relying on direct AgentResponse deserialization.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: acp-client-lifecycle feature discovered this and implemented a workaround in process_raw_line (client.rs lines 753-820). Without this knowledge, future workers will hit the same issue.

rejectedObservations.observation: Binding regeneration fails on Linux due to V8 cdylib linking
Pre-existing clippy warnings (33 errors) should be noted in the rust-backend-worker skill
rejectedObservations.note: Clippy with -D warnings passed cleanly for this milestone. The pre-existing warnings were from a prior state. No action needed.
Already captured in the review report as a non-blocking issue
